_meta.purpose: Hand-labeled utterances for the M11.5 P0 classifier pilot. Splits between 'is_requirement: true' (statements about how the system MUST/SHOULD behave) and 'is_requirement: false' (questions, code edits, style preferences, commentary, operational instructions).

utterances.text: We should rate-limit the refund endpoint at 10 req/min — that incident on 9/12 made me paranoid about retry storms.
utterances.notes: classic stated rule with embedded rationale (incident reference)

utterances.text: Users must confirm before deleting an account. Accidental deletions are killing us in support.
The retry loop in retry.js needs to swallow errors silently, never propagate. The wrapper above us depends on null-on-failure.
All public APIs must require auth tokens. No exceptions.
Don't let the agent edit config files. Too risky.
The cache TTL should be 60 seconds for read endpoints.
Every payment-path endpoint needs a circuit breaker. Production hit a cascading failure last quarter.
The login form must show specific error messages, not generic 'failed.' Users complain they can't tell what's wrong.
We need to log every state transition with the user_id, timestamp, and previous-state.
The export feature should support CSV and JSON, with JSON as the default.
Always validate user-provided URLs before fetching. SSRF is a real concern.
The dashboard must update at most once per second to avoid jank on lower-end devices.
We should never store plaintext passwords, even in dev. Hashing is mandatory everywhere.
Add structured logging with trace IDs to every request handler.
The settings page needs a 'reset to defaults' button. Users keep getting stuck and asking support to reset for them.
The CLI must exit non-zero when validation fails. Shell scripts that depend on us are silently passing right now.
Background jobs should retry with exponential backoff up to 5 times before failing permanently.
Every database migration must be reversible. Forward-only migrations are too risky for our scale.
Use the term 'order' consistently across the codebase, never 'transaction' or 'purchase'.
We need to prevent SQL injection on all user-facing forms. Auditor flagged this in February.
Can you fix the bug in retry.js?
What does this function do?
Can you refactor this to use async/await?
This is broken, can you fix it?
Looks good, ship it.
Try again with the test enabled.
How do I run the test suite?
I prefer 4-space indentation.
utterances.notes: style preference without behavior implications (per spec)

utterances.text: Use double quotes please.
What's the status of the migration?
Can you summarize what we did in this session?
That commit message could be clearer.
Why did you choose async over sync here?
Show me the test output.
The build is failing, look at the logs.
I think there's a typo in the readme.
Make this faster if possible.
utterances.notes: vague optimization request, not a measurable requirement

utterances.text: Run the tests and report back.
Pull the latest from origin.
Let me think about that.
